name: hset
description: Set a field in a hash stored at key with an optional expiration time. name: The Redis hash key. key: The field name inside the hash. value: The value to set. expire_seconds: Optional; time in seconds after which the key should expire. A success message or an error message.

key.description: The field name inside the hash.

expire_seconds.description: Optional; time in seconds after which the key should expire.

name: hget
description: Get the value of a field in a Redis hash. name: The Redis hash key. key: The field name inside the hash. The field value or an error message.

key.description: The field name inside the hash.

name: hdel
description: Delete a field from a Redis hash. name: The Redis hash key. key: The field name inside the hash. A success message or an error message.

key.description: The field name inside the hash.

name: hgetall
description: Get all fields and values from a Redis hash. name: The Redis hash key. A dictionary of field-value pairs or an error message.

name: hexists
description: Check if a field exists in a Redis hash. name: The Redis hash key. key: The field name inside the hash. True if the field exists, False otherwise.

key.description: The field name inside the hash.

name: set_vector_in_hash
description: Store a vector as a field in a Redis hash. name: The Redis hash key. vector_field: The field name inside the hash. Unless specifically required, use the default field name vector: The vector (list of numbers) to store in the hash. True if the vector was successfully stored, False otherwise.

vector.description: The vector (list of numbers) to store in the hash.

vector_field.description: The field name inside the hash. Unless specifically required, use the default field name

name: json_set
description: Set a JSON value in Redis at a given path with an optional expiration time. name: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored. path: The JSON path where the value should be set. value: The JSON value to store (as JSON string, or will be auto-converted). expire_seconds: Optional; time in seconds after which the key should expire. A success message or an error message.

name.description: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored.

path.description: The JSON path where the value should be set.

value.description: The JSON value to store (as JSON string, or will be auto-converted).

expire_seconds.description: Optional; time in seconds after which the key should expire.

name: json_get
description: Retrieve a JSON value from Redis at a given path. name: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored. path: The JSON path to retrieve (default: root '$'). The retrieved JSON value or an error message.

name.description: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored.

path.description: The JSON path to retrieve (default: root '$').

name: json_del
description: Delete a JSON value from Redis at a given path. name: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored. path: The JSON path to delete (default: root '$'). A success message or an error message.

name.description: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored.

path.description: The JSON path to delete (default: root '$').

name: lpush
description: Push a value onto the left of a Redis list and optionally set an expiration time.

name: rpush
description: Push a value onto the right of a Redis list and optionally set an expiration time.

name: lpop
description: Remove and return the first element from a Redis list.

name: rpop
description: Remove and return the last element from a Redis list.

name: lrange
description: Get elements from a Redis list within a specific range. str: A JSON string containing the list of elements or an error message.

name: llen
description: Get the length of a Redis list.

name: lrem
description: Remove elements from a Redis list. name: The name of the list count: Number of elements to remove (0 = all, positive = from head, negative = from tail) element: The element value to remove A string indicating the number of elements removed.

count.description: Number of elements to remove (0 = all, positive = from head, negative = from tail)

name: delete
description: Delete a Redis key. key (str): The key to delete. str: Confirmation message or an error message.

name: type
description: Returns the string representation of the type of the value stored at key key (str): The key to check. str: The type of key, or none when key doesn't exist

name: expire
description: Set an expiration time for a Redis key. name: The Redis key. expire_seconds: Time in seconds after which the key should expire. A success message or an error message.

expire_seconds.description: Time in seconds after which the key should expire.

name: rename
description: Renames a Redis key from old_key to new_key. old_key (str): The current name of the Redis key to rename. new_key (str): The new name to assign to the key. Dict[str, Any]: A dictionary containing the result of the operation. On success: {"status": "success", "message": "..."} On error: {"error": "..."}

name: scan_keys
description: Scan keys in the Redis database using the SCAN command (non-blocking, production-safe). ⚠️  IMPORTANT: This returns PARTIAL results from one iteration. Use scan_all_keys() to get ALL matching keys, or call this function multiple times with the returned cursor until cursor becomes 0. The SCAN command iterates through the keyspace in small chunks, making it safe to use on large databases without blocking other operations. pattern: Pattern to match keys against (default is "*" for all keys). Common patterns: "user:*", "cache:*", "*:123", etc. count: Hint for the number of keys to return per iteration (default 100). Redis may return more or fewer keys than this hint. cursor: The cursor position to start scanning from (0 to start from beginning). To continue scanning, use the cursor value returned from previous call. A dictionary containing: Or an error message if something goes wrong. Example usage: First call: scan_keys("user:*") -> returns cursor=1234, keys=[...], scan_complete=False Next call: scan_keys("user:*", cursor=1234) -> continues from where it left off Final call: returns cursor=0, scan_complete=True when done

pattern.description: Pattern to match keys against (default is "*" for all keys).

count.description: Hint for the number of keys to return per iteration (default 100).

cursor.description: The cursor position to start scanning from (0 to start from beginning).

name: scan_all_keys
description: Scan and return ALL keys matching a pattern using multiple SCAN iterations. This function automatically handles the SCAN cursor iteration to collect all matching keys. It's safer than KEYS * for large databases but will still collect all results in memory. ⚠️  WARNING: With very large datasets (millions of keys), this may consume significant memory. For large-scale operations, consider using scan_keys() with manual iteration instead. pattern: Pattern to match keys against (default is "*" for all keys). batch_size: Number of keys to scan per iteration (default 100). A list of all keys matching the pattern or an error message.

pattern.description: Pattern to match keys against (default is "*" for all keys).

batch_size.description: Number of keys to scan per iteration (default 100).

name: search_redis_documents
description: Search Redis documentation and knowledge base to learn about Redis concepts and use cases. This tool exposes updated and curated documentation, and must be invoked every time the user wants to learn more in areas including: **Common Use Cases:** question: The question about Redis concepts, data structures, features, or use cases Union[List[Dict[str, Any]], Dict[str, Any]]: A list of documentation results from the API, or a dict with an error message.

question.description: The question about Redis concepts, data structures, features, or use cases

name: publish
description: Publish a message to a Redis channel. channel: The Redis channel to publish to. message: The message to send. A success message or an error message.

channel.description: The Redis channel to publish to.

name: subscribe
description: Subscribe to a Redis channel. channel: The Redis channel to subscribe to. A success message or an error message.

channel.description: The Redis channel to subscribe to.

name: unsubscribe
description: Unsubscribe from a Redis channel. channel: The Redis channel to unsubscribe from. A success message or an error message.

channel.description: The Redis channel to unsubscribe from.

name: get_indexes
description: List of indexes in the Redis database str: A JSON string containing the list of indexes or an error message.

name: get_index_info
description: Retrieve schema and information about a specific Redis index using FT.INFO. index_name (str): The name of the index to retrieve information about. str: Information about the specified index or an error message.

name: get_indexed_keys_number
description: Retrieve the number of indexed keys by the index index_name (str): The name of the index to retrieve information about. str: Number of indexed keys as a string

name: create_vector_index_hash
description: Create a Redis 8 vector similarity index using HNSW on a Redis hash. This function sets up a Redis index for approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search using the HNSW algorithm and float32 vector embeddings. index_name: The name of the Redis index to create. Unless specifically required, use the default name for the index. prefix: The key prefix used to identify documents to index (e.g., 'doc:'). Unless specifically required, use the default prefix. vector_field: The name of the vector field to be indexed for similarity search. Unless specifically required, use the default field name dim: The dimensionality of the vectors stored under the vector_field. distance_metric: The distance function to use (e.g., 'COSINE', 'L2', 'IP'). A string indicating whether the index was created successfully or an error message.

index_name.description: The name of the Redis index to create. Unless specifically required, use the default name for the index.

prefix.description: The key prefix used to identify documents to index (e.g., 'doc:'). Unless specifically required, use the default prefix.

vector_field.description: The name of the vector field to be indexed for similarity search. Unless specifically required, use the default field name

dim.description: The dimensionality of the vectors stored under the vector_field.

name: vector_search_hash
description: Perform a KNN vector similarity search using Redis 8 or later version on vectors stored in hash data structures. query_vector: List of floats to use as the query vector. index_name: Name of the Redis index. Unless specifically specified, use the default index name. vector_field: Name of the indexed vector field. Unless specifically required, use the default field name k: Number of nearest neighbors to return. return_fields: List of fields to return (optional). A list of matched documents or an error message.

query_vector.description: List of floats to use as the query vector.

index_name.description: Name of the Redis index. Unless specifically specified, use the default index name.

vector_field.description: Name of the indexed vector field. Unless specifically required, use the default field name

k.description: Number of nearest neighbors to return.

name: hybrid_search
description: Perform a hybrid search combining a Redis filter expression with KNN vector similarity. Hybrid search pre-filters documents by metadata before ranking by vector similarity — the standard pattern for production RAG and semantic search pipelines. Filter expression examples: "*"                              → no filter, pure vector search (same as vector_search_hash) "@category:{news}"              → tag filter "@year:[2020 2024]"             → numeric range "@lang:{en} @year:[2022 +inf]"  → combined tag + range "@title:redis"                  → full-text match on a text field Full filter syntax: https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/interact/search-and-query/query/ query_vector:       List of floats to use as the query vector. filter_expression:  Redis filter expression to restrict candidates before KNN ranking. Defaults to '*' (no filter). index_name:         Name of the Redis index (default: 'vector_index'). vector_field:       Name of the indexed vector field (default: 'vector'). k:                  Number of nearest neighbors to return. return_fields:      Additional fields to include in results (optional). A list of matched documents with their similarity score, or an error message.

query_vector.description: List of floats to use as the query vector.

filter_expression.description: Redis filter expression to restrict candidates before KNN ranking.

vector_field.description: Name of the indexed vector field (default: 'vector').

k.description: Number of nearest neighbors to return.

return_fields.description: Additional fields to include in results (optional).

name: dbsize
description: Get the number of keys stored in the Redis database

name: info
description: Get Redis server information and statistics. section: The section of the info command (default, memory, cpu, etc.). A dictionary of server information or an error message.

section.description: The section of the info command (default, memory, cpu, etc.).

name: client_list
description: Get a list of connected clients to the Redis server.

name: sadd
description: Add a value to a Redis set with an optional expiration time. name: The Redis set key. value: The value to add to the set. expire_seconds: Optional; time in seconds after which the set should expire. A success message or an error message.

value.description: The value to add to the set.

expire_seconds.description: Optional; time in seconds after which the set should expire.

name: srem
description: Remove a value from a Redis set. name: The Redis set key. value: The value to remove from the set. A success message or an error message.

value.description: The value to remove from the set.

name: smembers
description: Get all members of a Redis set. name: The Redis set key. A list of values in the set or an error message.

name: zadd
description: Add a member to a Redis sorted set with an optional expiration time. key (str): The sorted set key. score (float): The score of the member. member (str): The member to add. expiration (int, optional): Expiration time in seconds. str: Confirmation message or an error message.

name: zrange
description: Retrieve a range of members from a Redis sorted set. key (str): The sorted set key. start (int): The starting index. end (int): The ending index. with_scores (bool, optional): Whether to include scores in the result. str: The sorted set members in the given range or an error message.

name: zrem
description: Remove a member from a Redis sorted set. key (str): The sorted set key. member (str): The member to remove. str: Confirmation message or an error message.

name: xadd
description: Add an entry to a Redis stream with an optional expiration time. key (str): The stream key. fields (dict): The fields and values for the stream entry. expiration (int, optional): Expiration time in seconds. str: The ID of the added entry or an error message.

name: xrange
description: Read entries from a Redis stream. key (str): The stream key. count (int, optional): Number of entries to retrieve. str: The retrieved stream entries or an error message.

name: xdel
description: Delete an entry from a Redis stream. key (str): The stream key. entry_id (str): The ID of the entry to delete. str: Confirmation message or an error message.

name: set
description: Set a Redis string value with an optional expiration time. key (str): The key to set. value (str, bytes, int, float, dict): The value to store. expiration (int, optional): Expiration time in seconds. str: Confirmation message or an error message.

name: get
description: Get a Redis string value. key (str): The key to retrieve. str, bytes: The stored value or an error message.
